title: viola sororia var. missouriensis
intro: Viola sororia var. missouriensis, also known as the Missouri violet or blue violet, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the viola genus and is native to the Midwest region of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as sore throats, bronchitis, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Viola sororia var. missouriensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and even exhibit potential antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or by mixing the plant material with other herbs to create topical treatments.
